name: Brake Fade Bait
summary: The illusion that more brake pressure keeps adding control on a long descent.
tell: The learner keeps asking for harder braking instead of earlier braking.
countermeasure: Downshift early and use controlled braking before speed grows.

name: Speed on Descent
summary: Treating gravity like free acceleration instead of a control problem.
tell: The learner thinks the car can be slowed later on the way down.
countermeasure: Reduce speed before the slope compounds the risk.

name: Late Turn-In
summary: Entering a hairpin too late and too fast to keep the line clean.
tell: The learner steers after the bend already tightens.
countermeasure: Enter earlier, slower, and with a stable wheel.

name: Fog Overspeed
summary: Driving faster than your sight line can safely support in haze.
tell: The learner keeps the same speed even when the road disappears.
countermeasure: Slow to the visible field and widen the following gap.

name: Shoulder Panic
summary: Jerking toward the shoulder as if any edge space is a safe answer.
tell: The learner tries to use the shoulder without checking width or surface.
countermeasure: Treat the shoulder as controlled recovery space, not an improvisation lane.

name: Gravel Commitment
summary: Treating loose gravel like the same grip as clean asphalt.
tell: The learner keeps steering and braking inputs too sharp on loose stone.
countermeasure: Ease the inputs and restore traction before asking for more turn.
